key_strengths: 2.5 million U.S. subscribers on a recurring subscription model across chronic conditions (ED, hair loss, skincare, mental health)
Most recognized direct-to-consumer telehealth brand with demonstrated platform stickiness
74% gross margins and $2.35B in 2025 revenue reflecting real scale
$630M cash and zero-coupon 2030 convertible notes provide near-term liquidity runway
Novo Nordisk distribution partnership creates a path to remain in the GLP-1 weight-loss market post-compounding ban
key_risks: DOJ criminal referral from HHS General Counsel — existential tail risk with 30-40% estimated probability and no known timeline
GLP-1 compounding revenue (~31% of 2025 revenue) eliminated by FDA ban with no proven replacement model
Free cash flow collapsed from $198M to $57M despite 59% revenue growth as capex surged 5x to $226M
Active SEC disclosure investigation into whether CEO's public characterizations of GLP-1 revenue were materially misleading
Amazon Pharmacy entered weight-loss clinical services in January 2026, commoditizing HIMS's primary remaining growth driver
red_flags: CEO sold $33M in stock in August 2025 at near-peak prices shortly before GLP-1 regulatory and SEC scrutiny became public
CEO publicly described GLP-1 as serving 'a small minority' of subscribers while it represented 38% of H1 2025 revenue
FCF collapsed from $198M to $57M on 59% revenue growth — FCF conversion fell below 50% of net income warning threshold
Net debt of ~$341M against $57M FCF equals ~6x net debt/FCF — company cannot repay 2030 convertible notes from current operations
DOJ referral from HHS General Counsel is not a routine inquiry — scope and timeline unknown
Business model that underpins the 2026 thesis (Novo partnership) did not exist 30 days before this analysis
buy_triggers: DOJ investigation resolves without criminal charges or material operational restrictions
Two consecutive quarters of post-compounding 'clean' revenue confirm mid-teens organic growth in legacy segments (hair loss, ED, skincare, mental health)
2026 capex guidance comes in below $100M, demonstrating FCF will converge toward EBITDA
sell_triggers: DOJ announces criminal charges against executives — breaks consumer trust thesis permanently; exit regardless of price
Novo Nordisk re-files patent infringement lawsuit and obtains injunctive relief restricting HIMS from GLP-1 distribution
Two consecutive quarters of declining subscriber count — indicates core platform is shrinking, not transitioning
valuation_summary: At ~$22/share (~$5B market cap, ~39x trailing earnings), the base case intrinsic value is approximately $19–20/share with no margin of safety, while the bear case implies ~$6.60/share (70% downside) carrying a 25–35% probability; justifying today's price requires four independent assumptions to hold simultaneously in a business model that did not exist 30 days ago.
source_summary: Analysis draws on web search results from February–March 2026, Hims & Hers Q4/FY2025 investor press releases, SEC EDGAR filings, MacroTrends and StockAnalysis financial data, analyst estimates from MarketBeat and TipRanks, and news coverage from STAT News, Fierce Healthcare, and Bloomberg; no user-provided context files were available.
